channel.title: IngestThis
channel.description: Articles, tutorials, and resources for Data Engineers, Scientists, Analysts, and Architects.

item.title: The Complete Guide to Agentic Coding Tools in 2026
item.description: A deep dive into the four categories of agentic coding tools dominating 2026 — CLI agents, desktop IDEs, 24/7 autonomous agents, and model routers. 40+ tools compared.

item.title: Agentic Lakehouse Concurrency and Isolation
item.description: Agentic writes need isolation contracts, not just write permissions.

item.title: What AI Is and Isnt: A Laypersons Guide to How LLMs Actually Work
item.description: AI is not magic or sentient. Learn what large language models actually do, how vectors and embeddings work, and why todays AI is pattern matching not thinking. 155 chars

item.title: Getting Started with AI for Free: Every Tool Google Gives You at No Cost
item.description: You already have access to powerful AI tools through your Google account and no subscription needed. Here is everything Google offers for free and how to use it today. 157 chars

item.title: ChatGPT and Claude: Which AI Service Should You Pay For
item.description: ChatGPT and Claude are the two leading AI assistants. Here is what each offers at every price tier, how to use their desktop apps and advanced features like Dispatch and Clips, and which one fits your workflow. 162 chars

item.title: A Tour of Specialized AI Tools: Music, Video, Images, and More
item.description: Beyond chatbots lie specialized AI tools for creating music, generating videos, and editing images. Here is a tour of the best tools in 2026 and how they fit into daily productivity. 157 chars

item.title: Going Advanced: Open Source Models, Hermes Agent, and Local AI
item.description: Tired of subscriptions and privacy concerns? Open source models and agent frameworks let you run AI on your own hardware. Here is how to get started with Hermes Agent, OpenCode, DeepSeek, and local Llama models. 166 chars

item.title: Anatomy of an Agentic Lakehouse
item.description: An Agentic Lakehouse is storage, catalog governance, semantic context, and agents working as one operating model.

item.title: Apache Iceberg v4 Roadmap and Metadata Trees
item.description: A deep technical breakdown of Apache Iceberg v4's proposed architecture: adaptive metadata trees, one-file commits, relative paths, column families, and what the Delta 5.0 convergence actually means for your data platform.

item.title: Lakehouse Context Layers with Atlan and Iceberg v3
item.description: The context layer explains what lakehouse data means, which is the part table formats do not solve alone.

item.title: Real-Time Agentic Analytics with ClickHouse
item.description: Real-time agents need analytical systems that can answer while an event still matters.

item.title: Composable Analytics Beats Metric Catalogs
item.description: Metric catalogs tell agents what terms mean. Composable analytics tells agents how to reason with those terms safely.

item.title: Goal-Directed Analytics Agents on Apache Iceberg
item.description: The next step after text-to-SQL is a governed action loop with checks before every external effect.

item.title: Iceberg Remote Signing for Regulated Datasets
item.description: Remote signing is the stricter pattern for lakehouse storage security because clients request signed file operations instead of receiving storage credentials.

item.title: Apache Iceberg v3 Deletion Vectors on Snowflake
item.description: Deletion vectors matter because row-level changes should not require a full rewrite of every affected data file.

item.title: CDC Without Complexity Using Iceberg v3 Row Lineage
item.description: Row lineage gives Iceberg a native way to tell incremental consumers which rows changed and when they changed.

item.title: The 2026 Guide to Iceberg View Federation
item.description: Portable views are the missing logic layer between open tables and multi-engine analytics.

item.title: Implementing MCP in the Lakehouse
item.description: MCP gives AI clients a standard way to call governed lakehouse tools instead of guessing how to query your data.

item.title: Microsoft Fabric Build 2026 Agentic Analytics Stack
item.description: Microsoft's Fabric direction shows that agentic analytics is becoming a platform architecture, not a chat feature.

item.title: Modern Python Tooling for Apache Iceberg
item.description: Python has become a practical Iceberg control plane for metadata work, catalog automation, and smaller operational workflows.
